title: The Mother and the Whore
review_summary: The reviewer finds Jean-Pierre Leaud's portrayal of an emotionally empty character who manipulates and hurts women profoundly realistic yet tragic; they praise his brilliant performance despite acknowledging that it's distressing for both characters involved and recommend watching it all at once in cinema.

title: The Garden of Allah
review_summary: Marlene Dietrich's performance shines despite an empty plot, praised cinematography but criticized editing; John Gilbert would have been idealized.

title: Rufus Jones for President
review_summary: The reviewer expresses strong disapproval of Rufus Jones for President as an African-American stereotype but appreciates Ethel Waters' singing performance; despite enjoying Sammy Davis Jr.'s tap-dancing skills and repeated enjoyment from hearing "I'll Be Glad When You're Dead You Rascal You," the film's negative stereotypes overshadow its entertainment value.

title: The Lady from Shanghai
review_summary: Orson Welles criticizes Harry Cohn’s edited version for its unoriginal musical choices and lackluster cuts that failed to enhance Rita Hayworth's performance; he finds Grigsby's character overshadowed.

title: Las Vegas Lady
review_summary: A disappointing blend of uninspired direction from Stella Stevens' gang leads them on an underwhelming money heist that fails despite potential plot elements; the 70s Las Vegas scenes don't compensate for its overall mediocrity and lackluster action, ultimately lacking in memorable moments or commendable performances.

The animation beautifully captures characters with lifelike charm despite their exaggerated forms; however, its unique floating island concept feels surreal but becomes believable within this imaginative context.

title: Doc Savage: The Man of Bronze
review_summary: A disappointing adaptation of 'Doc Savage' fails as an adventure classic due to its implausible protagonist lacking vulnerability; George Lucas likely wished he'd seen it before making Star Wars.

title: In a Year with 13 Moons
review_summary: The reviewer finds Fassbinder's work creatively interesting but overwhelmingly thinks his frequent movies and self-indulgence, particularly in gay/gender-bending themes like "QUERELLE," are poorly executed. They express disappointment that a potentially poignant message about gender confusion was mishandled with overly graphic scenes causing them to reconsider further exploration of Fassbinder’s films due to the negative experience outweighing positive ones.

Review highlights 'Murder By Numers' as an ultimately disappointing Hollywood movie marred by weak pacing and underutilized cast members; only its teen actors Ryan Gosling and Michael Pitt bring any notable merit to the forgettable script.
